pattern: A standard lower-bound binary search returns either the index of the target or the position where it would be inserted to keep the array sorted.
hints: The array is already sorted and distinct, so you never need to scan it linearly.
Think about binary search, but instead of giving up when the target is absent, track where it would belong.
Run a binary search for the first index whose value is >= target; that index is the answer whether the target exists or not.

edge_inputs.description: target smaller than every element inserts at zero

stress.description: sorted distinct array with a target that may or may not be present
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    vals = sorted(rng.sample(range(-10000, 10001), min(n, 20001)))
    target = rng.randint(-10001, 10001)
    return [vals, target]
stress.note: distinct guaranteed by sample; target range slightly exceeds values to hit both ends
